# Commitment Issues

A free developer tool that generates official-looking death certificates for abandoned GitHub repositories.

## What it does

- Paste any public GitHub repo URL to get a printable death certificate.
- Analyses commit history, archive status, and activity decay to assign a cause of death.
- Shows the last commit as "last words" and surfaces repo stats (stars, issues, language).
- Generates shareable certificate images and embeddable README badges.
- Profile graveyard scanner: paste a GitHub username to see every dead, struggling, and alive repo.

## Pages

- https://commitmentissues.dev — main tool, paste a repo URL or username.
- https://commitmentissues.dev/about — how it works, privacy, terms.
- https://commitmentissues.dev/legal — terms of use and privacy policy.
- https://commitmentissues.dev/user/[username] — graveyard report for a GitHub user.

## Public API (no auth)

- GET /api/repo?url={github_repo_url} — returns a full death certificate.
- GET /api/user?username={github_username} — returns repos grouped by Dead / Struggling / Alive.
- GET /api/badge?username={github_username} — SVG badge for GitHub READMEs.
- GET /api/certificate-image/{owner}/{repo} — Satori-rendered certificate PNG for embeds.
- GET /api/recent — the 10 most recent burials.
- GET /api/random — a random dead-repo URL.
- GET /api/stats — buried + profiles counters.
- GET /api/candle — wreath totals per repo.

## Notes

- Public repos only; no authentication required.
- Data sourced from GitHub's public REST API.
- Causes of death are algorithmic, not human-written.
- Free to use; no account required.
- Source: https://github.com/dotsystemsdevs/commitmentissues
